researched_category.notes: Notion is an all-in-one workspace platform for notes, documentation, wikis, databases, project management, and team collaboration.

one_liner.value: All-in-one workspace for notes, docs, wikis, databases, project management, and collaboration.
one_liner.notes: From Notion's own description on the pricing page and composio toolkit page.

methods.notes: Notion supports three auth models: Internal connections (static API token/bearer token), Public connections (OAuth 2.0), and Personal Access Tokens (user-scoped bearer token). All use the Authorization: Bearer header.

access.notes: Anyone can sign up for a free Notion account and create API credentials via the developer portal. The API quickstart says 'All you need is a Notion account and a terminal.' PAT creation may be restricted on Business/Enterprise plans by workspace owners, but the Free plan fully supports it.

summary.value: Notion provides a comprehensive REST API covering pages, blocks, databases (data sources), users, comments, search, file uploads, webhooks, and more. The API is versioned by date (currently 2026-03-11). Official JavaScript SDK (@notionhq/client) and community SDKs exist. Base URL: https://api.notion.com.

agent_interface.notes: Notion has an official hosted MCP server (mcp.notion.com) with OAuth authentication, plus an official open-source MCP server (@notionhq/notion-mcp-server on npm/GitHub, though soft-deprecated in favor of hosted). The hosted MCP server ships 18+ tools. Composio also provides a Notion toolkit with 48 tools and 8 triggers.

rationale.value: Notion is fully buildable as an agent toolkit today. It has a well-documented, broad, public REST API; official SDKs; an official hosted MCP server (remote) plus an open-source MCP server; self-serve free developer access with no paywall for API usage; and multiple authentication options (PATs, internal tokens, OAuth2) suitable for both interactive and headless agents. Composio already provides a Notion toolkit integration with 48 tools.

s1.quote: Notion supports three authentication models: Internal connections...use a static API token. Public connections use OAuth 2.0 for authentication. Personal access tokens (PATs) are user-scoped tokens...
s1.title: Authorization - Notion Docs

s2.quote: Notion connections let you connect your workspace to external tools and automate workflows through code. With the REST API, you can read, create, and update nearly everything in a workspace — pages, databases, users, comments, and more.
s2.title: Overview - Notion Docs

s3.quote: Requests use the HTTP Authorization header to both authenticate and authorize operations. The Notion API accepts bearer tokens in this header.
s3.title: Authentication - Notion Docs

s4.quote: Get up and running in under two minutes. All you need is a Notion account and a terminal.
s4.title: API quickstart - Notion Docs

s5.quote: Free — €0 per member / month. For individuals to organize personal projects and life.
s5.title: Notion Pricing Plans

s6.quote: A personal access token (PAT) lets you authenticate API requests as yourself. No connection setup or OAuth flow required.
s6.title: API quickstart - Notion Docs

s7.quote: The base URL to send all API requests is https://api.notion.com. HTTPS is required for all API requests. The Notion API follows RESTful conventions...
s7.title: Introduction - Notion Docs

s8.quote: [Lists extensive documentation covering] pages, blocks, databases (data sources), users, comments, search, webhooks, file uploads, views, and more.
s8.title: Notion Docs Index (llms.txt)

s10.quote: Notion MCP is our hosted server that gives AI tools secure access to your Notion workspace.
s10.title: Notion MCP - Notion Docs

s11.quote: Official MCP server for Notion API
s11.title: @notionhq/notion-mcp-server npm

s12.quote: Notion centralizes notes, docs, wikis, and tasks in a unified workspace. Tools: 48, Triggers: 8
s12.title: Notion - Composio Toolkit

limitations: The hosted Notion MCP server requires OAuth (browser-based flow) which may not work for fully headless/background agents; the open-source local MCP server is soft-deprecated. However, the direct REST API with PATs or internal integration tokens works fine for headless use.
PAT creation may be restricted on Business/Enterprise workspace plans unless enabled by workspace owners.
The API is free for now but the pricing page does not guarantee it will remain free forever; no current paywall exists for API access.
